label: Localized shoe contact rash without diabetic-foot or infection red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized shoe contact skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, ocular, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized shoe contact skin reaction with anaphylaxis, infection, mucosal, diabetic-foot, neurovascular-foot, severe-rash, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized shoe contact rash
discharge diagnosis localized shoe contact rash

high_confidence_terms: clinician diagnosis localized shoe contact rash

no infection, mucosal, or severe-rash red flags
